Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player account in the Axiom Frontier game, including their core profile and in-game currency balance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the player account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the player's last login.

axiomCoinBalance.description: The current balance of Axiom Coins, the primary in-game currency.

agentIds.description: References to Agent entities controlled by this player. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an in-game character controlled by a player, possessing unique stats, inventory, and behavior states.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who owns this agent. (Relationship: Agent N:1 Player)

type.description: The role or specialization of the agent (e.g., 'Gatherer', 'Warrior', 'Trader', 'Explorer').

currentLocation.description: A string representation of the agent's current position within the game world (e.g., 'Sector A-1, Quadrant 3').

energy.description: The agent's current energy or stamina points.

strength.description: The agent's strength attribute, affecting combat and gathering.

intelligence.description: The agent's intelligence attribute, affecting trading and exploration.

dexterity.description: The agent's dexterity attribute, affecting evasion and speed.

constitution.description: The agent's constitution attribute, affecting health and endurance.

behaviorState.description: The agent's current active behavior or AI state (e.g., 'gathering', 'combat', 'idle').

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Item entities currently in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

equippedItemIds.description: References to Item entities currently equipped by the agent. This is a subset of inventoryItemIds. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Defines an item within the game world, including purchasable goods, loot, and crafting materials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its purpose.

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item (e.g., 'Common', 'Rare', 'Epic', 'Legendary').

value.description: The monetary or trade value of the item in base game currency.

stackable.description: Indicates if multiple instances of this item can be stacked in inventory.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of this item that can be stacked together.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset entity representing the visual or audio asset for this item. (Relationship: Item N:1 Asset)

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents a quest or mission within the game, which can be dynamically generated by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest, potentially AI-generated.

status.description: The current status of the quest (e.g., 'Available', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Failed').

assignedToAgentId.description: Reference to the Agent entity to whom this quest is currently assigned. (Relationship: Quest 1:N Agent)

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item entities awarded upon completion of the quest. (Relationship: Quest N:N Item)

difficulty.description: A numerical value indicating the difficulty of the quest.

generatedByLLM.description: Indicates whether this quest's content (e.g., description) was generated by a Large Language Model.

youtubeScript.description: Optional: A generated YouTube script related to the quest's narrative or lore.

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a Non-Player Character in the game, which can have dynamically generated personalities and dialogue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

name.description: The display name of the NPC.

personalityDescription.description: A textual description of the NPC's personality, often dynamically generated by an LLM.

dialogueTreeId.description: Reference to a specific dialogue tree or script that defines the NPC's conversational responses.

location.description: A string representation of the NPC's current or primary location within the game world.

factionId.description: Identifier for the faction or group the NPC belongs to.

questIds.description: References to Quest entities that this NPC is involved with, either as a giver or a target. (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

GameWorldState.title: GameWorldState
GameWorldState.description: Represents global parameters and the overall state of the game world, including dynamic indices and economy metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameWorldState entity (expected to be a singleton, e.g., 'global_state').

civilizationIndex.description: A calculated index representing the overall development or health of civilizations in the world.

economyInflationRate.description: The current global inflation rate affecting the in-game economy.

lastWorldHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the last successful 'world heartbeat' cron job execution.

globalEventIds.description: References to active or scheduled global events affecting the game world.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of a financial transaction made by a player through the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who initiated the transaction. (Relationship: Transaction N:1 Player)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction.

currencyType.description: The type of currency used for the transaction (e.g., 'AxiomCoin', 'USD').

itemIds.description: References to Item entities purchased in this transaction. (Relationship: Transaction N:N Item)

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed').

paymentGatewayId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Manages references to in-game assets and textures stored in Firebase Storage for dynamic loading by the Godot client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the asset (e.g., 'SwordModel', 'ForestTexture').

storagePath.description: The URI path to the asset file in Firebase Storage.

version.description: A version string for the asset, used for updates and cache invalidation.

md5Hash.description: An MD5 hash of the asset file for integrity verification.

Civilization.title: Civilization
Civilization.description: Represents a distinct civilization within the game world, tracking its progress, characteristics, and influence.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Civilization entity.

name.description: The official name of the civilization.

indexValue.description: The calculated civilization index value, reflecting its overall status and power.

locationDescription.description: A textual description of the primary location or territory of the civilization.

governmentType.description: The type of government or social structure adopted by the civilization.

technologyLevel.description: A numerical representation of the civilization's technological advancement.

populationCount.description: The estimated population count of the civilization.

leaderNPCId.description: Reference to the NPC entity designated as the leader of this civilization. (Relationship: Civilization 1:1 NPC)

definition.description: Stores individual player profiles and core account information. Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `{playerId}`. Ensures QAPs for accessing player's own data.

Stores in-game agent data, owned by a specific player. Authorization is achieved by the hierarchical path where `{playerId}` must match `request.auth.uid`. This structure ensures Authorization Independence and supports QAPs for listing a player's agents.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who owns this Agent.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Agent.

definition.description: Records financial transactions made by a player. Authorization is based on the hierarchical path where `{playerId}` must match `request.auth.uid`. This provides Authorization Independence and enables QAPs for player-specific transaction history.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who made the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Transaction.

definition.description: Stores quest details assigned to a specific agent. Authorization is fully independent via the hierarchical path, where `{playerId}` must match `request.auth.uid` and `{agentId}` must belong to that player. This structure supports QAPs for listing quests for a player's agent.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who owns the Agent assigned to this Quest.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Agent to whom this Quest is assigned.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Quest.

definition.description: A global catalog of all in-game items. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, typically read-only for all authenticated players and writeable by administrators. Supports QAPs for listing all available items.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Item.

definition.description: A global catalog of all game assets (models, textures, sounds). This collection has a homogeneous security posture, typically read-only for all authenticated players and writeable by administrators. Supports QAPs for listing all available assets.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Asset.

definition.description: A global catalog of Non-Player Characters. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, typically read-only for all authenticated players and writeable by administrators. Supports QAPs for listing all NPCs.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier for the NPC.

definition.description: A global catalog of in-game civilizations. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, typically read-only for all authenticated players and writeable by administrators. Supports QAPs for listing all civilizations.

params.name: civilizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Civilization.

definition.description: A singleton document (e.g., `stateId` = 'global_state') holding global game parameters and world state. Typically read-only for all authenticated players and writeable only by administrators or specific Cloud Functions. Supports QAPs for accessing the single global state.

params.name: stateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the GameWorldState (expected to be 'global_state' for a singleton).

definition.description: A dedicated collection for storing UIDs of users who possess administrative privileges. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) via existence checks (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`).

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of an administrative user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, scalable, and debuggable security rules. All user-specific data (Players, Agents, Transactions, Quests) is structured hierarchically under the `/players/{playerId}` path. This path-based ownership is critical for Authorization Independence: a user's authenticated `uid` is directly matched against the `{playerId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents or external entities to determine access rights. For example, an Agent document at `/players/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}` implicitly asserts that the `Agent` belongs to the `Player` defined by the `playerId` in the path, which must match `request.auth.uid`. Similarly, `Quest` entities are nested under `/players/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}/quests/{questId}`, directly linking them to a specific agent owned by a specific player, making their authorization context self-contained within their path.

This hierarchical, path-based ownership directly facilitates QAPs (Queries as Permissions) for user-owned data. When a player queries `/players/$(request.auth.uid)/agents`, Firestore rules can easily filter and return only the agents owned by the authenticated player. The same applies to `/players/$(request.auth.uid)/transactions` and `/players/$(request.auth.uid)/agents/{agentId}/quests`. This structural enforcement ensures that `list` operations are inherently secure without requiring client-side filtering or complex server-side rule logic, as rules can simply check if `request.auth.uid == playerId` for all documents in these subcollections. Denormalization of authorization context is achieved by placing documents in paths that reflect their ownership, making the `playerId` attribute redundant as a primary authorization field within the document itself, while retaining it for query indexing if needed.

Global entities like `Items`, `Assets`, `NPCs`, `Civilizations`, and `GameWorldState` are stored in top-level collections. These collections adhere to Structural Segregation by having a homogeneous security posture: read-only for all authenticated players, and writeable only by users with an explicit 'admin' role, which is managed via the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. This clear separation of concerns simplifies security rules further, as global data does not have per-document ownership logic. The `GameWorldState` is designed as a singleton document (`/gameWorldState/global_state`) for centralized management of global game parameters. Overall, this design minimizes rule complexity, prevents atomic operation breakdowns, and maximizes debuggability by making access intent explicit through the data structure itself.
